channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Last of Us season 2's release date has been set for mid-April, and I'm certain that I know when its next trailer will arrive
item.description: HBO has confirmed that its hugely popular dystopian drama series will return in less than two months.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: La lezione del low-code che i CIO possono applicare all’agentic AI
item.description: Con il passare del tempo, la GenAI si sta trasformando sempre di più da un chatbot a cui si fanno domande, a un set di strumenti ai quali si chiede di fare qualcosa e l’agentic AI [in inglese] – l’espressione un po’ goffa con la quale si definisce questo approccio – sta rendendo sempre più chiaro ch
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft unveils company's first quantum computing chip
item.description: Microsoft on Wednesday revealed its first quantum computing chip, Majorana 1, stating it will speed up the development of quantum computers able to solve "meaningful, industrial-scale problems in years, not decades.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: This AI tool could help curb domestic violence
item.description: A new technology can pinpoint victims of intimate partner violence four years earlier than other detection systems and with 80% accuracy.
item.dc:creator: Sibani Ram, Fast Company

item.title: Movie sales – including 4K Blu-ray – fell again last year, but if you're going streaming only, you're massively missing out
item.description: Physical media sales, including 4K Blu-ray, have once again declined, but it's easily still the best way to watch your favorite movies and TV shows at home.
item.dc:creator: james.davidson@futurenet.com (James Davidson), TechRadar

item.title: Apple unveils a souped-up and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone
item.description: Apple has released a sleeker and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone in an attempt to widen the audience for a bundle of artificial intelligence technology that the company has been hoping will revive demand for its most profitable product lineup.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Venture Capital kills ‘more value than it creates,’ Bay Area author says
item.description: In a new book, World Eaters, tech activist Catherine Bracy says Silicon Valley needs to focus more on solving real-world problems and less on fast profit.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Smart home device maker exposes 2.7 billion records in huge data breach
item.description: Major IoT data breach: Mars Hydro database with 2.7 billion records exposed. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says the breach is a reminder of the risks that come with using IoT devices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: I’ve got two fresh iPhone 17 rumors for you, but one of them stinks
item.description: New iPhone 17 rumors: Samsung displays tipped for all models, along with a crazy design change for the iPhone 17 Pro Max.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Office 2021 and 2024 cheat sheet
item.description: For decades, Microsoft Office has been the business world’s dominant productivity software suite. If you use word processing, spreadsheet, presentation, and email applications, particularly on Windows, those apps are very likely to be Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI cofounder Ilya Sutskever’s new AI startup is fundraising with a $30 billion valuation
item.description: A new artificial intelligence company from one of the cofounders of OpenAI is quickly becoming one of the most highly valued AI firms in an increasingly crowded marketplace.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Experts give up hope for 157 false killer whales stranded on a beach in Australia's Tasmania state
item.description: Marine experts have given up hope of rescuing more than 150 false killer whales that stranded on a remote beach on Australia’s island state of Tasmania.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Virginia Tech researchers raise red flags about mixed-reality security
item.description: In a new study, researchers at Virginia Tech spelled out a variety of security hazards that could compromise mixed-reality systems. The researchers highlighted security threats involving the manipulation of virtual objects when users collaborated via mixed reality headsets.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The White House Denies Elon Musk's Control Over DOGE - A Surprising Twist in the Ongoing Power Struggle
item.description: Elon Musk's role in DOGE, a mysterious government department, has been called into question by 14 US states. The White House now claims he is not in charge, sparking further confusion and concern.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Burchett compares DOGE staffers to young WWII soldiers
item.description: Rep. Tim Burchett (R-Tenn.) on Monday compared Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffers to young World War II soldiers. Burchett was pressed by CNN’s Pamela Brown on DOGE’s reported attempt to enter an IRS system, as well as its overall access to data.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Data center spending to top $1 trillion by 2029 as AI transforms infrastructure
item.description: Massive global demand for AI technology is causing data centers to increase spending on servers, power, and cooling infrastructure. As a result, data center CapEx spending will hit $1.1 trillion by 2029, up from $430 billion in 2024, according to Dell’Oro Group. Much of this growth is due to AI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump and EU Commission set course for confrontation on big tech
item.description: width="2193" height="1234" sizes="(max-width: 2193px) 100vw, 2193px">With the second Trump administration, very different cultures are once again clashing in the transatlantic relationship. rawf8 – shutterstock.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Musk’s xAI launches Grok 3, expanding AI capabilities with deep search and reasoning
item.description: Elon Musk’s AI startup xAI has introduced Grok 3, the latest version of its chatbot model, which Musk describes as the most advanced AI system yet.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Neudesic launches in Australia in a bid to speed up AI adoption for IT teams
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) has gained significant traction among business leaders keen to explore ways it can drive operational efficiencies and cost savings. But while top leadership is sold on its potential, it’s a different tale for IT teams working the ground.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: For February’s Patch Tuesday, Microsoft rolls out 63 updates
item.description: Microsoft released 63 patches for Windows, Microsoft Office, and developer platforms in this week’s Patch Tuesday update. The February release was a relatively light update, but it comes with significant testing requirements for networking and remote desktop environments.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
